description: The system shall display notes in a hierarchical tree based on dot-separated paths.
The system shall allow users to expand and collapse tree nodes.
The system shall open a note when its node is selected in the tree.
The system shall generate virtual child nodes based on schema rules and user-defined patterns.
The system shall visually distinguish virtual nodes from existing notes.
The system shall allow users to create a note from a virtual node.
The system shall persist tree expansion and collapse state between sessions.
The system shall allow users to rename an existing note directly from the tree.
The system shall display the frontmatter title instead of the filename when a title field is present.
The system shall display files of all types supported by Obsidian (not limited to Markdown files) within the tree.
The system shall allow users to expand all nodes in the hierarchical tree via a single action.
The system shall allow users to collapse all nodes in the hierarchical tree via a single action.
The system shall allow users to open the closest existing parent note of the currently selected node.
The system shall allow users to reveal the currently open file within the tree view.
The system shall allow users to create a new child note from a selected node using a dedicated command.
The system shall allow users to rename a note and all its descendant notes in a single operation.
The system shall support horizontal scrolling to accommodate deeply nested hierarchies in the tree view.
The system shall load schema rules from a configuration file (JSON or Markdown format).
The system shall match files against schema patterns to generate virtual child suggestions.
The system shall support exclusion patterns to prevent suggestions for specific file patterns.
The system shall support hierarchical suggestions using dotted child names (e.g.
The system shall prevent renaming a note to a name that already exists and display an appropriate error message.
The system shall allow users to undo the last rename operation.
The system shall allow users to cancel a rename operation in progress and rollback completed changes.
The system shall display folders alongside dot-separated notes in the hierarchical tree.
The system shall display the hierarchical tree with no perceptible delay for the user on typical vault sizes.
The system shall remain responsive when handling large vaults (10
The system shall support fast startup through cached data to minimize initial load time.
The system shall remain usable on both desktop and mobile environments.
The system shall allow smooth navigation of deeply nested hierarchies without blocking interactions.
The system shall be compatible with supported versions of Obsidian community plugins.
The system shall not corrupt or delete existing user notes during normal operation.
The system shall preserve user data integrity across sessions and restarts.
